title: AMC Theatres Trims Quarterly Loss, Overall Revenues Rise
text:
AMC Theatre Third Quarter 2024 Financial Analysis

AMC reported mixed results for Q3 2024, with revenues reaching $1.35 billion but posting a smaller net loss due to operational improvements. The quarter demonstrated the theater chain's ability to drive revenue growth while managing costs more effectively than in prior periods.

Key Performance Metrics: Total revenues reached $1,348.8 million, representing a 31% increase from Q2 2024. However, the company posted a net loss of $20.7 million, though this was a significant improvement—narrowing by 37% compared to the previous quarter's loss of $33 million.

Operational Highlights: The company achieved record third-quarter admissions revenue per patron and all-time record food and beverage revenue per patron. Notably, AMC matched pre-pandemic Q3 2019 Adjusted EBITDA levels despite 25% lower attendance than that period, demonstrating significant operational leverage.

Debt Management Progress: CEO Adam Aron highlighted debt management success: Our debt transactions announcement in July pushed the maturity of $2.4 billion of our long-term debt out into the future for several years, from 2026 to 2029 and 2030. Year-to-date, AMC reduced outstanding debt by $349 million through capital markets activities.

Forward Outlook: Management expressed optimism about upcoming theatrical releases, expecting industry-wide box office growth through 2026. The company's strategy of extending debt maturities while improving per-patron economics provides a foundation for potential recovery.
